sections.label: Navigation Bar
sections.instruction: This is the strip at the very top of your page. Put your handle and brand here — visitors see this first.

fields.name: handle
fields.label: Username / Handle
fields.instruction: Write your username or handle. This shows in the OS status bar at the top of your portfolio.

fields.name: logo
fields.label: Monogram / Short ID
fields.instruction: Use 2–3 characters as your logo mark — usually your initials. Shows in the top-left corner.

fields.name: systemName
fields.label: OS / System Name
fields.instruction: A creative name for your portfolio OS. Shows in the center of the top bar. Keep it short and techy.

sections.label: Hero Section (Your Big Intro)
sections.instruction: This is the first big thing visitors see — your name, title, photo, and a short intro. Make it confident and clear.

fields.name: title
fields.label: What You Do (Job Title)
fields.instruction: Your role in a few words. This shows up big and bold under your name.

fields.name: subtitle
fields.label: Your Skills (Short Version)
fields.instruction: A quick line listing your main skills. Separate them with dots or commas.

fields.name: bio
fields.label: About You (Short Bio)
fields.placeholder: I architect systems that scale to millions and ship tools developers actually love. 7 years turning caffeine into reliable infrastructure.
fields.instruction: Write 2–3 sentences about yourself. What do you build? What makes you good at it? Be confident — this is your pitch.

fields.name: avatar
fields.label: Your Photo
fields.instruction: Upload one clear photo of yourself. It will appear inside an animated frame on the page.

fields.name: statusBadge
fields.label: Availability Status Badge
fields.instruction: A small floating label near your photo showing your availability. Keep it short — e.g. 'Open to work · Remote'.

fields.name: resumeLink
fields.label: Link to Your CV / Resume
fields.instruction: Paste the full link to your CV. Upload it to Google Drive and share the link. Must start with https://

fields.name: yearsExp
fields.label: Years of Experience
fields.instruction: Just the number. Shows as a floating stat badge near your photo.

fields.name: projectsCount
fields.label: Projects Shipped
fields.instruction: How many projects have you built? Shows as another floating badge.

fields.name: coffeeCount
fields.label: Fun Stat Label
fields.instruction: A third floating badge for a fun or creative stat about yourself. Keep it light.

sections.label: Skills
sections.instruction: Group your skills by category. Each group becomes one glowing panel. You can add up to 8 groups. Example groups: Core Languages, Frontend, Backend, Tools.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Skill Category Name
fields.instruction: What type of skills are in this group? e.g. Core Languages, Frontend, Backend, DevOps, Tools.

fields.name: skills
fields.label: Skills in This Category
fields.instruction: List your skills separated by commas. These appear as glowing tags on the panel.

sections.label: Work Experience
sections.instruction: List the jobs you've had. Each job becomes a timeline entry in the OS log view. Put newest jobs first. If you have no experience yet, just turn the section off below.

fields.name: showExperience
fields.label: Show Work Experience Section?
fields.instruction: Turn this OFF if you have no work experience yet. The section will be completely hidden from your portfolio.

fields.name: role
fields.label: Your Job Title
fields.instruction: What was your role? e.g. Junior Developer, Principal Engineer, Freelance Developer, Intern.

fields.name: company
fields.label: Company or Client Name
fields.instruction: Name of the company or client. If you freelanced, write the client's name or just 'Self-employed'.

fields.name: period
fields.label: When Did You Work There?
fields.instruction: Write the time period naturally. e.g. 'Jan 2022 – Present' or '2021 – 2023'.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Location
fields.instruction: City name or 'Remote' if you worked online.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Did You Do There?
fields.placeholder: Owned the edge runtime infrastructure powering 3M+ deployments/day. Reduced cold start latency by 70% through custom V8 isolate pooling.
fields.instruction: Describe your work in 2–3 sentences. What did you build? What did you achieve? Numbers help — e.g. 'reduced latency by 70%'.

sections.label: Projects
sections.instruction: Your main work showcase. Each project gets an interactive OS window card with an image, description, and links. Add up to 12 projects. Your best work goes first.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Project Name
fields.instruction: The name of your project.

fields.name: date
fields.label: Year Built
fields.instruction: Just the year, or a range like '2023 – 2024'.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Is This Project?
fields.placeholder: An adaptive caching layer using ML to predict cache eviction patterns, achieving 94% hit rate on production workloads.
fields.instruction: Explain what the project does and what makes it interesting. 2–3 sentences max. What problem does it solve?

fields.name: image
fields.label: Project Screenshot
fields.instruction: Upload a screenshot or preview image of your project. Wide (landscape) images look best. This is the first thing visitors notice on the card.

fields.name: tags
fields.label: Technologies Used
fields.instruction: What did you build it with? Separate with commas.

fields.name: github
fields.label: GitHub Link
fields.instruction: Link to the project's GitHub repository. Only add this if the repo is public.

fields.name: webapp
fields.label: Live Project Link
fields.instruction: Link to the live hosted version of your project. If it's not hosted yet, leave this empty.

fields.name: showMembers
fields.label: Did You Build This With a Team?
fields.instruction: Turn ON if this was a group project. You can then add your teammates' names and GitHub links.

sections.label: Education
sections.instruction: Your academic background. Each entry becomes a card in the OS directory view. Online courses count too — add them if you have no formal degree. Up to 6 entries.

fields.name: degree
fields.label: Degree or Course Name
fields.instruction: The full name of your degree or course. e.g. 'M.Sc. Computer Science' or 'Full Stack Web Dev Bootcamp (Udemy)'.

fields.name: institution
fields.label: University, College, or Platform
fields.instruction: Where did you study? For online courses, write the platform name e.g. Udemy, Coursera.

fields.name: period
fields.label: When Did You Study?
fields.instruction: Write the years. e.g. '2020 – 2024' or just '2023' for a short course.

fields.name: grade
fields.label: Grade or Distinction (Optional)
fields.instruction: Your grade, GPA, or any honours. Skip this field if you'd rather not show it.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Extra Notes (Optional)
fields.placeholder: Specialization in distributed systems. Thesis: Self-healing microservice meshes using reinforcement learning.
fields.instruction: Anything extra worth mentioning — your thesis topic, a club, a specialization, or a major achievement.

sections.label: Certifications
sections.instruction: Certificates you've earned — Udemy, Coursera, Google, CNCF, anything counts. Each one becomes a verified achievement tile. Up to 12 certifications.

fields.name: showCertifications
fields.label: Show Certifications Section?
fields.instruction: Turn this OFF if you have no certifications. The section will be hidden from your portfolio.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Certificate Name
fields.instruction: The official name of the certificate exactly as it appears on the certificate.

fields.name: issuer
fields.label: Who Gave You This Certificate?
fields.instruction: The platform or organization that issued it. e.g. Udemy, Google, Coursera, CNCF, Meta.

fields.name: date
fields.label: When Did You Get It?
fields.instruction: Month and year is enough. e.g. 'November 2023'.

fields.name: link
fields.label: Verification Link (Optional)
fields.instruction: The shareable link to verify your certificate. Udemy, Coursera, and Credly give you one after completion. Paste it here so people can check it.

sections.label: Contact
sections.instruction: How people get in touch with you. At minimum, add your email. Only add social links for platforms you actually use.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Your Email Address
fields.instruction: Use an email you actually check. This is how clients will contact you.

fields.name: github
fields.label: GitHub Profile Link
fields.instruction: Your full GitHub profile URL. e.g. https://github.com/alexdev

fields.name: showTwitter
fields.label: Show Twitter on Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Turn OFF if you don't want Twitter shown.

fields.name: showFacebook
fields.label: Show Facebook on Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Turn ON if you want Facebook shown.

fields.name: showInstagram
fields.label: Show Instagram on Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Turn ON if you want Instagram shown.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Your Location
fields.instruction: Your city or 'Remote' if you work online. Clients like knowing where you're based.

fields.name: availability
fields.label: Availability Note
fields.placeholder: Available for freelance & full-time roles from Jan 2025
fields.instruction: A short friendly line that appears above your contact info. Think of it as an invitation to reach out.

sections.label: Footer
sections.instruction: The very last line at the bottom of your page. Usually your name and the year.

fields.name: text
fields.label: Footer Text
fields.placeholder: Designed & built by Alex Devlin — no frameworks were harmed.
fields.instruction: One short line. Keep it simple — your name and year is enough, or add a fun personal touch.
